5 min read
1 hour ago

--

Learn the intuition behind Prefix Sum, recognize it in interviews, and solve most subarray sum problems with confidence.
If youвҖҷve solved a few array problems on LeetCode, youвҖҷve probably noticed that many of them ask similar questions:

вҖў Find the sum of a subarray.

вҖў Count subarrays whose sum equals K.

вҖў Find subarrays divisible by K.

вҖў Answer thousands of range sum queries efficiently.
At first glance, these problems look completely different.
But once you understand Prefix Sum, youвҖҷll realize they are all built on the same fundamental idea.

Why Do We Need Prefix Sum?
Imagine this array:
nums = [2, 4, 1, 5, 3]Now suppose I ask:
What is the sum of elements from index 1 to index 3?
The straightforward approach is:
4 + 1 + 5 = 10Time Complexity:
O(length of subarray)Nothing wrong with that.
But now imagine I ask this question 100,000 times.
Your program keeps adding the same numbers again and again.
ThatвҖҷs a huge waste of work.
There must be a better approach.

The Core Idea
Instead of recalculating subarray sums repeatedly,
calculate the running total once.
This running total is called the Prefix Sum.
Once we know the cumulative sum at every position,
every subarray sum becomes a simple subtraction.

A Real-Life Analogy
Imagine your bank account.
Instead of remembering every single transaction,
your bank shows your current balance.
Suppose you want to know:
вҖңHow much money did I spend between Monday and Friday?вҖқ
You donвҖҷt add every transaction manually.
You simply calculate:
Balance before Friday
вҲ’
Balance before MondayExactly the same idea is used in Prefix Sum.
Instead of storing every subarray,
we store the cumulative sum seen so far.

Building the Prefix Sum Array
Original array
Index : 0 1 2 3 4Value : 2 4 1 5 3Now calculate cumulative sums.
prefix[0] = 0
prefix[1] = 2
prefix[2] = 6
prefix[3] = 7
prefix[4] = 12
prefix[5] = 15Final Prefix Array
Index0 1 2 3 4 5Prefix0 2 6 7 12 15Notice something important.
The prefix array has one extra element.
That extra 0 makes the mathematics much cleaner.
Each position stores:
The sum of all elements before that index.

Why Does This Work?
Suppose we need
Sum(1...3)That means
4 + 1 + 5Instead of adding again,
look at the prefix array.
prefix[4] = 12prefix[1] = 2Now subtract
12 вҲ’ 2 = 10Done.
No loop.
No repeated computation.
Just one subtraction.

The One Formula You Should Remember
Sum(L...R) = Prefix[R + 1] вҲ’ Prefix[L]ThatвҖҷs the entire Prefix Sum pattern.
Everything else is simply a variation of this formula.

Why R + 1?
Many beginners get confused here.
Remember this definition:
prefix[i]= sum of elements before index iSo
prefix[R+1] contains everything until index R.Subtract
prefix[L]which contains everything before L.
The remaining part is exactly
L ... R
Master Template
Every Prefix Sum problem starts with building the prefix array.
int n = nums.length;
long[] prefix = new long[n + 1];
for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
prefix[i + 1] = prefix[i] + nums[i];
}To calculate any range sum,
long sum = prefix[R + 1] - prefix[L];Time Complexity
Building : O(n)
Query : O(1)Space Complexity
O(n)
Dry Run
Array
[3,1,4,2]Build Prefix
0
3
4
8
10Now find
Sum(1,3)Using the formula
Prefix[4]вҲ’Prefix[1]= 10вҲ’3 = 7Which equals
1+4+2=7
How Do You Recognize Prefix Sum?
Whenever you read a problem,
ask yourself one simple question.
Am I repeatedly calculating the sum of subarrays?
If the answer is Yes,
Prefix Sum should immediately come to mind.
Common interview keywords include:

вҖў Range Sum

вҖў Subarray Sum

вҖў Continuous Subarray

вҖў Count Subarrays

вҖў Sum Equals K

вҖў Divisible by K

вҖў Multiple Queries

The Three Major Prefix Sum Patterns
Most interview questions belong to one of these three categories.

Pattern 1 вҖ” Range Sum Query
Question
Find the sum between L and R.
Solution
long answer = prefix[R + 1] - prefix[L];Examples
Range Sum Query вҖ” Immutable
Multiple Range Sum Queries

Pattern 2 вҖ” Prefix Sum + HashMap вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ
This is the most important variation.
Question . Count subarrays whose sum equals K.
Suppose current prefix is
15Need
Subarray Sum = 7Question
Is there an earlier prefix
8because
15вҲ’8=7If yes,
then the elements between those two prefixes sum to 7.
Instead of storing array elements,
store
Prefix Sum вҶ’ Frequency
Master Template
Map<Long, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
map.put(0L, 1);
long prefix = 0;
int answer = 0;
for (int num : nums) {
prefix += num;
answer += map.getOrDefault(prefix - k, 0);
map.put(prefix,
map.getOrDefault(prefix, 0) + 1);
}Problems

вҖў 560 Subarray Sum Equals K

вҖў 930 Binary Subarrays With Sum

вҖў 1248 Count Number of Nice Subarrays

Pattern 3 вҖ” Prefix Sum + Modulo
Question
Count subarrays whose sum is divisible by K.
Observation
If
Prefix1 % K == Prefix2 % K
then
Prefix2 вҲ’ Prefix1
is divisible by K.Instead of storing the prefix sum,
store
Prefix % K
Master Template
Map<Integer, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
map.put(0,1);
int prefix = 0;
int answer = 0;
for(int num : nums){
prefix = (prefix + num) % k;
if(prefix < 0)
prefix += k;
answer += map.getOrDefault(prefix,0);
map.put(prefix,
map.getOrDefault(prefix,0)+1);
}Problems

вҖў 974 Subarray Sums Divisible by K

Bonus вҖ” 2D Prefix Sum
The same idea extends to matrices.
Instead of storing cumulative sums for an array,
store cumulative sums for a grid.
Rectangle Sum Formula
P[r2+1][c2+1]вҲ’
P[r1][c2+1]
вҲ’
P[r2+1][c1]
+
P[r1][c1]Problem

вҖў 304 Range Sum Query 2D

2D Prefix Sum
Common Interview Mistakes
1. Wrong Formula
Incorrect
prefix[R]вҲ’prefix[L]Correct
prefix[R+1]вҲ’prefix[L]
2. Forgetting
map.put(0,1);Without this,
subarrays starting from index 0 will never be counted.

3. Negative Modulo
Always normalize.
prefix %= k;if(prefix < 0)
prefix += k;
4. Integer Overflow
For large arrays,
always prefer
long[] prefix;instead of
int[] prefix;
Complexity
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Practice Problems
Easy

вҖў Range Sum Query вҖ” Immutable

вҖў Running Sum of 1D Array
Medium

вҖў Subarray Sum Equals K вӯҗ

вҖў Binary Subarrays With Sum

вҖў Subarray Sums Divisible by K

вҖў Contiguous Array

вҖў Count Number of Nice Subarrays
Hard

вҖў Number of Submatrices That Sum to Target

Final Mental Model
Think of the Prefix Sum array as a running balance.
Instead of asking,
вҖңWhat is the sum of this subarray?вҖқ
ask,
вҖңHow much has the total sum increased between these two positions?вҖқ
That shift in thinking changes the problem from repeated addition to a single subtraction.
Once this intuition clicks, Prefix Sum stops being just another algorithm and becomes a natural way to approach almost every subarray-sum problem youвҖҷll encounter in coding interviews.
